_**User (2026-04-16 11:32:48Z)**_

I am navigating my rptototype and am currently in "/routes". What is the main file that contains what should be displayed in a higher hierarchy? Explain it to me as if I only understant HTML and CSS code structures, but not React neither Vite.

---

_**Agent (copilot/gpt-4.1)**_

In your prototype, when you are at the "/routes" page, the main file responsible for what you see is src/pages/MapRoutes.tsx.

Think of this file like the "main content" section of a traditional HTML page. In classic HTML, you might have something like:

```html
<body>
  <!-- header, nav, etc. -->
  <main>
    <!-- Main content for this page -->
  </main>
  <!-- footer, etc. -->
</body>
```

In your project, src/pages/MapRoutes.tsx is like the <main> part for the "/routes" page. It controls what is shown in the main area when you visit that route.

- It does not include the global header, navigation bar, or footer (those are usually handled by other files, like a layout or app wrapper).
- It only defines what should appear in the main content area for "/routes".

So, if you want to change what is displayed when you visit "/routes", you should edit src/pages/MapRoutes.tsx. This file uses React (a JavaScript library) to build the page, but you can think of it as the main "section" for this route, similar to how you would edit the <main> content in an HTML file.

---

_**User (2026-04-16 11:35:40Z)**_

Inside MapRoutes.tsx, how can I know that it defines "/routes"? In a HTML file there is a clear definition of it at the header, but I do not see it in MapRoutes when looking at a glance

---

_**Agent (copilot/gpt-4.1)**_

Great question! In React projects (especially those using Vite and React Router), the connection between a file like src/pages/MapRoutes.tsx and the URL path (such as "/routes") is not written directly inside the file itself. Instead, this connection is made in a separate configuration file where all the routes are defined.

In classic HTML, you see the page’s address in the file name or in a <base> or <a href=""> tag. In React, the file MapRoutes.tsx is just a component (like a template or a section), and another file (often called App.tsx or a routes file) tells the app: “When the user visits /routes, show the MapRoutes component.”

So, you won’t see "/routes" written inside MapRoutes.tsx. Instead, you would find something like this in another file (for example, src/App.tsx or a routes config):

```jsx
<Route path="/routes" element={<MapRoutes />} />
```

This line means: “When the user goes to /routes, display what’s inside MapRoutes.tsx.”

Summary:
- MapRoutes.tsx only describes what to show.
- The actual URL path ("/routes") is mapped to this file in a separate routing configuration file.
- To find this, look for a file where <Route path="/routes" ...> is used.

If you want to know exactly where this happens in your project, I can help you find the file that defines this mapping!
